lenses.guidance: vv 15-23's cloud-and-fire threads from Ex 13:21-22 through 1 Kgs 8:10-11's temple cloud to Mt 17:5's transfiguration cloud and Acts 2:3's tongues of fire. The pillar of visibility runs throughout Scripture as God's mobile presence.

vv 15-16's cloud over the tabernacle is the visible kavod (glory). Jn 1:14's logos that 'tabernacled among us' takes up the same image; Acts 1:9's cloud receives Christ at the ascension. Jesus inhabits and inherits Numbers' cloud.

vv 17-23's pattern — 'when the cloud lifted, the Israelites set out; wherever the cloud settled, they encamped' — trains the worshipper's posture today. To trust is to wait when nothing moves; to obey is to break camp when it does.

vv 6-13's Second Passover — provision for those defiled or absent — is the chapter's hidden mercy. The covenant feast opens a second time so no Israelite forfeits redemption by accident. The arc of grace bends toward the distant.
